emails.subject: Q4 2026 Compliance Review — Coordinators, Please Read
emails.body_text:
Team,

As a reminder, Q4 2026 internal quality review will sample closed cases from the Oct–Dec quarter. Please ensure all case files closed during September and October 2026 are fully uploaded to the shared repository by November 14.

No action required until November 14. This is an informational notice.

Tamra Reyes
Director, Appeals & Grievances
Mojave Crest Assurance Company

emails.subject: Appeal of Denied Claim — Patient Elena Novak (DOB 04/12/1979)
emails.body_text:
To the Mojave Crest Appeals Unit,

Southwest Desert Orthopedics is writing to formally appeal the denial of claim CLM-2026-041903 for patient Elena Novak (Member ID VA-31802-04, DOB 04/12/1979).

Claim details:
  Date of service: October 6, 2026
  CPT codes: 29881 (arthroscopy, knee, surgical), 29876
  Place of service: 24 (ambulatory surgical center)
  Billed amount: $14,200.00
  Denial reason: 'Services not medically necessary'

We believe this denial is incorrect and are appealing on behalf of our patient. The procedure was medically necessary given the member's documented degenerative joint disease (M17.31) and failure of conservative treatment over the preceding eight months. Supporting clinical records are attached.

Please direct any questions to our billing coordinator at the address above.

Respectfully,
Michael Osei, Billing Director
Southwest Desert Orthopedics
4820 E. Desert Ridge Dr., Phoenix AZ 85016
appeals@swdesertortho.com | (480) 555-0177

emails.subject: Coverage Concern — Joanna Fierro — Member ID VA-55219-07
emails.body_text:
To Mojave Crest Appeals,

I am writing to appeal the termination of my residential treatment coverage at Desert Oaks Recovery Center. I received a termination notice dated November 1, 2026 stating that my coverage will end on November 5, 2026 at 10:00 AM.

I have been in residential substance use disorder treatment since October 20, 2026 under Authorization #AUTH-2026-04819. I am still actively receiving care and my treatment team believes I am not yet ready for a lower level of care.

I am appealing this termination urgently. I believe the criteria your plan used to decide I no longer need residential treatment are stricter than what you would apply to someone needing medical inpatient rehabilitation — for example, your criteria for medical rehab patients allow more time for clinical progress than your criteria for someone like me in SUD residential. That doesn't seem fair or legal to me.

Please respond immediately. My coverage ends in less than 48 hours and I have nowhere to go.

Joanna Fierro
Member ID: VA-55219-07
Date of Birth: 03/17/1988
Phone: (702) 555-0231

emails.subject: Appeal — Denied Claim for Inpatient Rehabilitation Feb 2024 — Roberto Casillas
emails.body_text:
To Mojave Crest Assurance,

My name is Roberto Casillas. My member ID is VA-88291-03 and my group is G-14827. I am writing to appeal a claim denial I received in the mail.

Background: I was hospitalized at Desert Sky Rehabilitation Center from February 12 to February 14, 2024 for medical rehabilitation following a workplace injury. Mojave Crest paid this claim in April 2024. However, I recently received a notice dated May 15, 2026 telling me that Mojave Crest has reversed this payment and is now denying the claim, citing 'medical necessity criteria not met per MP-089.'

I was surprised to receive this notice more than two years after my treatment. I believe the care I received was medically necessary — I was seriously injured and my doctor required that I have in-patient rehabilitation. I have attached the original hospital records and my doctor's notes.

Please review this appeal and let me know what happens next. The denial letter said I have 180 days from May 15, 2026 to appeal, so I believe I am within that window.

Sincerely,
Roberto Casillas
Member ID: VA-88291-03
DOB: 11/02/1971
Phone: (702) 555-0388
r.casillas.personal@gmail.com

emails.subject: RE: Case CASE-2026-00437 — Follow-Up Question About My Claim
emails.body_text:
Hello,

I received a letter about my open appeal Case #CASE-2026-00437. I wanted to contact you because I looked at the claim that was denied and something is wrong.

The claim from Desert Peak Specialty Group says I was seen on October 12, 2026. I was NOT at their office on October 12. I checked my calendar and I was working out of town that week. I have never been seen by Desert Peak Specialty Group — I don't even know who they are.

Someone is billing for services I never received. I am very concerned about this. Can you please look into it? I don't want to be held responsible for a bill for something that didn't happen.

Daniela Torres
Member ID: VA-62883-11
Phone: (702) 555-0419

emails.subject: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00437 - Acknowledgment of Your Appeal
emails.body_text:
Dear Ms. Torres,

This letter confirms that Mojave Crest Assurance Company has received your appeal regarding the denial of neurology and EEG services billed by Desert Peak Specialty Group (CPT codes 99244 and 95819, date of service October 12, 2026).

  Case Number: CASE-2026-00437
  Member ID: VA-62883-11
  Date Received: October 14, 2026
  Appeal Type: Post-Service Standard
  Decision Due By: December 13, 2026

Sincerely,
Jordan Mills
Appeals & Grievances Coordinator
Mojave Crest Assurance Company
Phone: (702) 555-0148
Case #: CASE-2026-00437
